prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with NaiveSeasonal dominating seasonal capture while ONLY_CWT_catboost leads trend tracking. Early horizons favor rf/ETS/ARIMA while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost/DWT_rf/THETA, confirming that model diversity is essential. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the forecast horizon.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_CWT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "ETS",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "rf", "late": "ONLY_DWT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ETS", "THETA", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "DWT_rf", "catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["FT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "DWT_svr", "FT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9718,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with NaiveSeasonal dominating seasonal capture while ONLY_CWT_catboost leads trend tracking. Early horizons favor rf/ETS/ARIMA while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost/DWT_rf/THETA, confirming that model diversity is essential. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the forecast horizon."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.272 exceeds 0.25 threshold, favoring robust methods like dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). n_windows=3 requires small top_k for stability; shrinkage=0.35 adds regularization. RMSE spread ratio=0.9718 shows extreme disagreement between best (ETS=2380.87) and worst (svr=4469.77) models, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern insights confirm ONLY_CWT_catboost as trend_champion and NaiveSeasonal as seasonality_champion, supporting diverse ensemble composition."
}
raw.statistician:
{
  "add_names": ["topk_mean_per_horizon_k4", "best_single_by_validation"],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "1. relative_spread_mean=0.272 ≥0.25 indicates high disagreement - current weighted/DBA methods are appropriate. 2. n_unique_winners=9 ≥3 supports per-horizon selection diversity. 3. Best candidate has rmse_std_over_rmse=0.485 >0.3 indicating instability - need more robust candidates. 4. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 (SMAPE=0.25316) beats current k3 (SMAPE=0.25499). best_single_by_validation (RMSE=2747.65) beats current best (RMSE=2776.06). 5. n_windows=3 is low - recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization but we keep current shrinkage=0.35. 6. Current set has no pure baseline mean/median, so adding best_single_by_validation provides a simple robust alternative.",
  "changes": ["added topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 for better SMAPE performance", "added best_single_by_validation as robust single-model fallback"],
  "when_good": "candidate set is stable (rmse_std_over_rmse <0.3) and relative_spread_mean <0.25"
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.97) and high seasonality variance (0.79), with NaiveSeasonal dominating seasonal capture while ONLY_CWT_catboost leads trend tracking. Early horizons favor rf/ETS/ARIMA while late horizons prefer ONLY_DWT_catboost/DWT_rf/THETA, confirming that model diversity is essential. Given both high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the forecast horizon.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=9 indicates high model diversity across horizons. relative_spread_mean=0.272 exceeds 0.25 threshold, favoring robust methods like dba_combination (recommended_method_hint). n_windows=3 requires small top_k for stability; shrinkage=0.35 adds regularization. RMSE spread ratio=0.9718 shows extreme disagreement between best (ETS=2380.87) and worst (svr=4469.77) models, justifying inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. Pattern insights confirm ONLY_CWT_catboost as trend_champion and NaiveSeasonal as seasonality_champion, supporting diverse ensemble composition.

statistician.rationale: 1. relative_spread_mean=0.272 ≥0.25 indicates high disagreement - current weighted/DBA methods are appropriate. 2. n_unique_winners=9 ≥3 supports per-horizon selection diversity. 3. Best candidate has rmse_std_over_rmse=0.485 >0.3 indicating instability - need more robust candidates. 4. Universe leaderboards show topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 (SMAPE=0.25316) beats current k3 (SMAPE=0.25499). best_single_by_validation (RMSE=2747.65) beats current best (RMSE=2776.06). 5. n_windows=3 is low - recommended_knobs suggest stronger regularization but we keep current shrinkage=0.35. 6. Current set has no pure baseline mean/median, so adding best_single_by_validation provides a simple robust alternative.
changes: added topk_mean_per_horizon_k4 for better SMAPE performance
added best_single_by_validation as robust single-model fallback
statistician.when_good: candidate set is stable (rmse_std_over_rmse <0.3) and relative_spread_mean <0.25
